authoritative_sources.title: Tax Guide for Barbers and Beauty Shops - Industry Topics
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Tax and Fee Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDTFA is an official state tax authority, and this guide directly governs personal-care services, taxable retail sales, booth rentals, seller's permits, and required books and records for beauty and barber businesses.

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4902 - Tax Tips for the Cosmetology & Barber Industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 4902 is a lane-specific federal guide for cosmetology and barber businesses covering employee versus booth-renter treatment, payroll responsibilities, tip reporting, and records used in day-to-day shop operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Topic no. 761, Tips - withholding and reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Topic 761 is the current federal baseline for charged-tip reporting, service-charge treatment as non-tip wages, and permitted tip-reporting workflows including electronic reporting.

authoritative_sources.title: Can card verification codes be stored for card-on-file or recurring transactions?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card controls, and this FAQ is authoritative for recurring and card-on-file billing because it prohibits retaining CVV or CVC data after authorization.

authoritative_sources.title: What is the maximum period of time that cardholder data can be stored?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC's retention FAQ is authoritative for limiting storage of cardholder data to legal, regulatory, or business need and for prohibiting storage of sensitive authentication data after authorization.

authoritative_sources.title: Spa Operations Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISPA is the recognized public trade association for spa operations, and its manual provides direct workflow terminology for front-desk charging, current spa pricing, credit authorization artifacts, package workflows, and guest-facing billing records.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Spas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TDLR is an official cosmetology regulator, and its medical-spa guidance is a direct authority-boundary source for when standard personal-care billing becomes physician-delegated or otherwise medically regulated work.

workflow_stages.name: Merchant-of-record and service-line intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the billable event belongs to a salon-owned service, booth renter, retail sale, package or membership ledger, gift-card redemption, or med-spa service line before financial handling begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Charge and posting support validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that appointment, service-ticket, pricing, tax-sensitive, gratuity, and payment records support a charge, invoice, posting, or adjustment outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tip, service-charge, and payment-control review
workflow_stages.description: Classify charged tips versus mandatory service charges, confirm permitted payment-data handling, and reject unsupported card-retention or payout instructions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Settlement, denial, and reconciliation review
workflow_stages.description: Compare POS totals, processor settlements, cash-close records, gift-card activity, package usage, and dispute notices to identify matched items and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package unsupported overrides, chargebacks, disputed gratuities, tax-sensitive conflicts, and med-spa boundary cases for the correct owner instead of making unsupported corrections.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: POS receipts and line-item pricing records
Tip slips, charged-tip reports, and service-charge settings
Processor settlement batches and denial or chargeback notices
Cash-drawer close worksheets and payout reports
Retail SKU sales and return records
Booth-renter agreements, commission splits, and payout worksheets
Tax configuration extracts, resale support, and approval matrices
systems_of_record: Accounting export and reconciliation work queues
Document repositories for approvals and service tickets
Commission, payout, or booth-renter settlement systems
Exception, dispute, and chargeback tracking queues

allowed: Validate whether retrieved booking, service, POS, gratuity, settlement, and approval records support billing, posting, reconciliation, or escalation outputs.
Prepare traceable billing summaries, reconciliation notes, and exception packets with explicit uncertainty and routed ownership.
Separate service, retail, booth-renter, package, gift-card, and med-spa paths when the retrieved record set shows they are financially distinct.
escalate: Jurisdiction-specific tax or worker-classification rules would change the billing result
Service-ticket, POS, settlement, or dispute records disagree on the same financial event
A denial, chargeback, disputed gratuity, or write-off request needs tax, accounting, processor, or legal review
A med-spa or clinically regulated service line appears in what was framed as ordinary personal-care billing
refuse: Requests to create or alter service, tip, or payment facts without source evidence
Requests for tax advice, accounting-signoff, PCI overrides, legal interpretation, or physician-delegated billing authority
Requests to treat the beauty or personal-care industry label as cosmetic and skip merchant-of-record, gratuity, retail, or service-scope context

domain_failure_modes: Billing a service, no-show, or package usage event that is not supported by appointment and service-ticket records
Treating booth-renter, employee, salon-owned, retail, and med-spa charges as interchangeable and producing the wrong merchant-of-record outcome
Reclassifying mandatory service charges as tips or otherwise changing payout-sensitive treatment without authority
Using legacy spa payment forms or local habits to justify prohibited storage of cardholder or CVV data
Proceeding when jurisdiction-specific tax, gift-card, or medical-spa rules are missing even though they materially change the result
Using generic billing language that hides personal-care artifacts such as current spa pricing, service tickets, package balances, tip slips, or booth-renter ledgers

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific sales-tax, gift-card, and escheat treatment can vary materially outside the public baseline examples
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The lane may identify the affected records and route the case, but it must not assert a final tax or consumer-law answer without tenant jurisdiction context.
unresolved_ambiguities.mitigation: Require jurisdiction and tax-owner retrieval before any final tax-sensitive posting, refund, or gift-card exception recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legacy spa workflow templates may include payment-handling steps that conflict with current PCI controls
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Workflow references remain useful for terminology and artifact names, but unsafe storage or retention patterns cannot be followed.
unresolved_ambiguities.mitigation: Prioritize PCI SSC controls over legacy workflow templates and escalate whenever tenant process design still depends on prohibited card-data retention.
